channel.title: Posts on Phuong Anh's Corner
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Phuong Anh's Corner

item.title: 🔬 When Open-Source AI Meets Qualitative Research: Trust, Privacy, and the Limits of LLM-Assisted Coding
item.description:
Based on research by Tung T. Ngo, Dai Nguyen Van, Anh-Minh Nguyen, Phuong-Anh Do, and Anh Nguyen-Quoc, 2026

Qualitative data analysis is one of the most intellectually demanding tasks in the social sciences. Open coding — the painstaking process of reading through interview transcripts, field notes, or documents and assigning conceptual labels to segments of meaning — can take weeks or months for even a modestly sized dataset. So when large language models (LLMs) started showing real capability at language tasks, qualitative researchers understandably took notice. The question isn’t whether AI can speed up this work; it’s whether researchers can trust it enough to actually use it, and whether doing so compromises the rigour that makes qualitative research valuable in the first place.

item.title: 📊 Learning Outcome Assessment in the Age of AI: Rethinking What We Measure and How
item.description:
Learning Outcome Assessment in the Age of AI: Rethinking What We Measure and How

Based on research by Do Phuong Anh and Vu Thi Lan, 2025

As AI tools become ubiquitous in educational settings, we’re facing a fundamental crisis in how we assess student learning. The traditional metrics we’ve relied on for decades—standardized test scores, essay quality, problem-solving accuracy—are all being disrupted by technologies that can perform these tasks as well as or better than many students. This isn’t just a technical challenge; it’s forcing us to confront deeper questions about what learning actually means and how we can meaningfully measure it.

item.title: 🎯 Maintaining Pedagogical Integrity in AI-Enhanced Classrooms: Beyond the Technology Hype
item.description:
Maintaining Pedagogical Integrity in AI-Enhanced Classrooms: Beyond the Technology Hype

Based on research by Do Phuong Anh and Nguyen Thi Phuong Thao, 2025

As AI tools flood into classrooms worldwide, there’s a critical question that most educators are wrestling with but few are addressing head-on: How do we maintain the fundamental principles of good teaching when everything around us seems to be changing? While the media celebrates AI as the great educational disruptor, those of us actually working in classrooms know the reality is far more complex.

item.title: 🏫 AI and English Dialogic Learning: Key Takeaways from a Conference on Technology in Higher Education
item.description:
AI and English Dialogic Learning: Key Takeaways from a Conference on Technology in Higher Education

Based on a conference presentation by Do Phuong Anh, 2025

At the Faculty of English for Specific Purposes conference at Foreign Trade University, a presentation synthesized current research on integrating AI into English dialogic learning for higher education contexts. The session drew together two of the most consequential trends in education today: the global demand for English language proficiency and the rapid rise of Artificial Intelligence. What follows is a summary of the key arguments and evidence presented.

item.title: 💬 Integrating AI in English Dialogic Learning: Roles, Benefits, and a Human-Centered Path Forward
item.description:
Integrating AI in English Dialogic Learning: Roles, Benefits, and a Human-Centered Path Forward

Based on research by Do Phuong Anh, 2025

The quest for English language proficiency has always driven pedagogical innovation. Dialogic learning—which treats language acquisition as a fundamentally social, collaborative process—has emerged as one of the most promising frameworks for developing genuine communicative competence [1]. Now, the rapid advance of Artificial Intelligence is reshaping what dialogic learning can look like, who (or what) can participate in it, and how educators should position themselves within it.

item.title: 🗣️ How English Language Teachers Perceive AI-Facilitated Dialogic Learning: Benefits, Concerns, and a New Framework
item.description:
How English Language Teachers Perceive AI-Facilitated Dialogic Learning: Benefits, Concerns, and a New Framework

Based on research by Do Phuong Anh, 2025

As AI tools permeate every corner of professional life, English language teaching faces a particular moment of reckoning. The core of communicative language pedagogy has always been dialogue—real, messy, unpredictable human exchange. Now a technology capable of engaging in that exchange is arriving in classrooms worldwide. What do teachers actually think about it?

item.title: Publications
item.description:
Journal Articles & Conference Papers

2026

  1. Ngo, T. T., Nguyen Van, D., Nguyen, A.-M., Do, P. A., & Nguyen-Quoc, A. (2026). Qualitative Coding Analysis through Open-Source Large Language Models: A User Study and Design Recommendations. Extended Abstracts of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI EA ‘26), April 13–17, 2026, Barcelona, Spain. ACM. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2602.18352 PDF

2025

  1. Do, P. A., & Vu, T. (2025). Learning Outcome Assessment in the Age of AI. PDF

item.title: 📽️ Redesigning Assessments and Curriculum for AI-Era Learners
item.description:
Originally presented by Do Phuong Anh and Nguyen Thi Phuong Thao, April 21, 2025

Last year, in my previous blog post on the challenges of integrating AI into English language education, I emphasized that one of the strategies for successful integration is rethinking our assessment methods. With breathless proclamations about AI revolutionizing learning, here’s the reality check educators need: while AI tools are undeniably transformative, the path forward isn’t about jumping on the hype train—it’s about making thoughtful, strategic changes to how we assess and teach students, so that we can embrace authentic language use.

item.title: 👋 I am Phuong Anh
item.description:
Introduction

Hi! 👋 I am an educator 👩🏻‍🏫, tech enthusiast 🤖, and cat-lover 🐈

item.title: 🤖 The Promise and Peril of AI in English Language Education: What Educators Need to Know
item.description:
Since ChatGPT burst onto the scene in November 2022 [18], educators worldwide have been grappling with a fundamental question: What are Large Language Models (LLMs) and How do we integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) into English language teaching?

In the recent Symposium on Using AI in Foreign Language Teaching hosted by FTU’s Faculty of ESP, I wrote a short paper that tries to go against of this hype and provide a balanced view of the challenges that AI presents in language education. This post summarizes the key points from that paper, which is available in full here.
